title: The mac version of gvproxy runs for a while and stops forwarding
body:
### Issue Description

Start the virtual machine in debug mode. After a period of time, the podman ssh virtual machine cannot enter, and podman ps does not respond. I think it should be the problem of gvproxy forwarding, because the graphical interface of qemu still exists and can operate the virtual machine



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman machine start --log-level=debug 
2.A day later, podman machine ssh  or podman ps 


### Describe the results you received

its stuck

### Describe the results you expected

normal response

### podman info output

comments.body:
➜  ~ podman machine ssh --log-level=debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
DEBU[0000] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/x/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p 65064 root@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL=]
kex_exchange_identification: read: Connection reset by peer
DEBU[0010] Called machine ssh.PersistentPostRunE(podman machine ssh --log-level=debug)
DEBU[0010] Shutting down engines

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19182789/230762063-e52537de-f19a-4890-92bd-a966a954b335.png)
When a malfunction occurs, executing the following command will restore the network. From this, it can be concluded that the dhcp lease of gvproxy has expired
ifconfig enp0s1 down
 ifconfig enp0s1 up

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19182789/230764542-aa0b8ed9-07ff-4a3f-9b0b-7afdd9593793.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19182789/230764704-7baf4705-a99d-4a99-b007-b220232eeb43.png)
Send dns request at 52211 seconds, then lost IP address at 53181 seconds
This problem occurs in about 14 hours

In the end, it was found that there was still a problem with the fedora coreos of the virtual machine. When this problem occurs, I can solve this problem by restarting the network card or manually setting the ip and gateway in the qemu graphical interface

NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="37.20230401.2.0 (CoreOS)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=37
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f37"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora CoreOS 37.20230401.2.0"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:37"
HOME_URL="https://getfedora.org/coreos/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=37
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=37
SUPPORT_END=2023-11-14
VARIANT="CoreOS"
VARIANT_ID=coreos
OSTREE_VERSION='37.20230401.2.0'

I'm going to re-initialize a stable virtual machine, because the default initialization is a test branch virtual machine. I think there may be a problem with the Neworkmanager used by the test branch for dhcp

still have a problem

Combined with the packet capture analysis, the IP address was lost, resulting in the failure to send the DHCP lease renewal message.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19182789/230814671-34054f31-ffd0-49a3-bf0f-3b105349f698.png)

After an afternoon of observation, it is determined that it is a problem with the qemu simulator, and has nothing to do with dhcp.

https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/1365
